perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, upon 60 days' written notice, by the vote of a majority of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or by the vote of the Fund's Trustees or by the Blockchain Administrator.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, upon 10 days' written notice, by the vote of a majority of the outstanding voting securities of the Fund or by the vote of the Fund's Trustees or by the Blockchain Administrator.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the termination notice period from 60 days to 10 days may conflict with the Investment Company Act of 1940, which requires a reasonable notice period for terminating investment advisory contracts.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 15(a)(3) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 requires that contracts with investment advisors (like the Blockchain Administrator here) continue for a reasonable period and can be terminated with reasonable notice. While the statute doesn't define 'reasonable,' a reduction to 10 days could be deemed unreasonable, especially given the complexities of winding down administration of digital securities and smart contracts. This modification introduces a risk of non-compliance with the implicit requirement of a reasonable notice period for orderly termination of the agreement.
perturbation.location: 7. Duration and Termination of this Agreement (a)

Contracts of advisers and underwriters (a) Written contract to serve or act as investment adviser; contents It shall be unlawful for any person to serve or act as investment adviser of a registered investment company, except pursuant to a written contract, which contract, whether with such registered company or with an investment adviser of such registered company, has
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the termination notice period from 60 days to 10 days, which the Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation argue may violate the Investment Company Act of 1940. Section 15(a)(3) implicitly requires a reasonable notice period for terminating investment advisory contracts. The shortened notice period could be deemed unreasonable, thus creating a contradiction because it doesn't comply with the implicit requirement of a reasonable notice. Scraped Law Snippet 2 shows that 15 USC 80a-15 is associated with the Investment Company Act of 1940, meaning the change may violate this act.

perturbation.original_text: In compliance with the requirements of Rule 31a-3 under the Investment Company Act, the Blockchain Administrator agrees that all records which it maintains for the Fund shall at all times remain the property of the Fund, shall be readily accessible during normal business hours, and shall be promptly surrendered upon the termination of the Agreement or otherwise on written request.
perturbation.changed_text: In compliance with the requirements of Rule 31a-3 under the Investment Company Act, the Blockchain Administrator agrees that all records which it maintains for the Fund shall at all times remain the property of the Blockchain Administrator, shall be accessible with reasonable notice, and shall be surrendered only upon a court order.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the ownership of records and the conditions for surrender conflicts with SEC Rule 31a-3 under the Investment Company Act of 1940.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 270.31a-3 explicitly states that records maintained by service providers like the Blockchain Administrator are the property of the fund and must be readily accessible and promptly surrendered. The modified text violates this rule by asserting the Blockchain Administrator's ownership, limiting accessibility, and requiring a court order for surrender, directly contradicting the SEC regulation intended to protect fund assets and shareholder interests.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 270.31a-3 - Records prepared or maintained by other than person required to maintain and preserve them. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 270.31a-3 - Records prepared or maintained by other than person required to maintain and preserve them. CFR prev | next § 270.31a-3 Records prepared or maintained by other than person required to maintain and preserve them. (a) If the records required to be maintained and preserved pursuant to the provisions of §§ 270.31a-1 and 270.31a-2 are prepared or maintained by others on behalf of the person required to maintain and preserve such records, the person required to maintain and preserve such records shall obtain from such other person an agreement in writing to the effect that such records are the property of the person required to maintain and preserve such records and will be surrendered promptly on request. (b) In cases where a bank or member of a national securities exchange acts as custodian, transfer agent, or dividend disbursing agent, compliance with this section shall be considered to have been met if such bank or exchange member agrees in writing to make any records relating to such service available upon request and to preserve for the periods prescribed in § 270.31a-2 any such records as are required to be maintained by § 270.31a-1 . (Sec. 31, 54 Stat. 838 ; 15 U.S.C. 80a-30 ) [ 27 FR 11994 , Dec. 5, 1962] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts 17 CFR § 270.31a-3. The original text ensures the fund owns the records and can promptly access them. The changed text gives ownership to the Blockchain Administrator, limits accessibility with 'reasonable notice,' and requires a court order for surrender. Snippet 2, 'the person required to maintain and preserve such records shall obtain from such other person an agreement in writing to the effect that such records are the property of the person required to maintain and preserve such records and will be surrendered promptly on request,' confirms that the fund should own the records and have prompt access. The modification fundamentally alters ownership and access, creating a clear and serious contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Such fee shall be accrued daily and paid monthly in arrears.
perturbation.changed_text: Such fee shall be accrued annually and paid at the Blockchain Administrator's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment schedule and discretion introduces a potential conflict with the Investment Company Act of 1940 and related SEC guidance regarding reasonable and transparent fee arrangements.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 15(c) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 requires fund directors to request and evaluate information reasonably necessary to evaluate the terms of the advisory agreement. Changing from a monthly payment schedule to payment at the Blockchain Administrator's discretion introduces uncertainty and lack of transparency regarding fees. This violates the implied requirement that advisory fee arrangements must be sufficiently transparent to allow fund directors to assess their reasonableness as per Section 15(c).
perturbation.location: 4. Compensation; Allocation of Costs and Expenses (a)

Contracts of advisers and underwriters (a) Written contract to serve or act as investment adviser; contents It shall be unlawful for any person to serve or act as investment adviser of a registered investment company, except pursuant to a written contract, which contract, whether with such registered company or with an investment adviser of such registered company, has
perturbation.justification: The modified text shifts the fee accrual from 'daily' to 'annually' and payment from 'monthly in arrears' to 'at the Blockchain Administrator's discretion.' This introduces uncertainty and a lack of transparency in fee arrangements, potentially conflicting with the implied requirement in Section 15(c) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 that advisory fee arrangements must be sufficiently transparent to allow fund directors to assess their reasonableness. The original contract ensures predictable and regular payments, while the changed text grants the Blockchain Administrator significant control over the timing of payments, creating ambiguity and potentially hindering the ability of fund directors to evaluate the terms of the advisory agreement effectively. Scraped Law Snippet 1 references 15 USC 80a-15, which addresses contracts of advisers and underwriters, supporting the relevance of the Investment Company Act of 1940 to the contract's terms. Scraped Law Snippet 2 also lists 15 USC 80a-15, reinforcing its importance.
